### /Users/tsavo/Projects/neurallog/examples/inventory.ts:checkAvailability (line 11)

Proven properties (Z3 confirmed unsat):
  [P5] PROVEN: available at line 11 equals the return value of db.getStock (assignment identity)

Known violations (Z3 confirmed sat):
  [P3] VIOLATION: db.getStock has no lower-bound contract; available can be negative
  [P2] VIOLATION: reserveStock mutates setAvailable but never setStock.
  [P2] VIOLATION: releaseStock mutates setAvailable (adds back) but never setStock.
  [P6] VIOLATION: available = 0 is reachable; checkAvailability returns 0 with no error signal.

### /Users/tsavo/Projects/neurallog/examples/inventory.ts:reserveStock (line 18)

Proven properties (Z3 confirmed unsat):
  [P5] PROVEN: At line 18, `available` and `reserved` are exactly the return

Known violations (Z3 confirmed sat):
  [P7] VIOLATION: available - quantity underflows (oversell).
  [P3] VIOLATION: quantity is caller-supplied with no positivity check before line 18.
  [P3] VIOLATION: negative quantity inverts reserveStock semantics.
  [P4] VIOLATION: Two calls to reserveStock on the same productId.
  [P2] VIOLATION: checkAvailability reads db.getStock; reserveStock reads db.getAvailable.
